exit_criteria: Knowledge docs reflect accepted claim wait queue requirements.

loop_start.reason: Compiler loops start from source refs and build handoffs, not prior loop chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Fresh validation is preferred; policy may require it for high-risk semantic gates.

next_loop.reason: Implementation should validate from artifacts and tests.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset
Documentation build and TASK-074 carry implementation context.
next_loop.handoff: Use TASK-074 and this documentation build for claim wait queue implementation.

requirements.text: CodeWiki claims must support a wait queue for sessions that cannot safely claim needed scopes because another active write claim blocks them.
Waiting sessions must have their own TTL/heartbeat so dead waiters expire and do not create stale wakeups.
When blocking claims release or expire, matching waiters should become ready and be visible through claim state/graph/status so adapters can resume from artifacts or start a fresh handoff.
Wake behavior must not revive stale chat context as truth; resumed work starts from task/build/scope artifacts and current graph state.

evidence_mapping.criterion: Claim wait queue semantics are documented.
evidence_mapping.evidence: .codewiki/kb/system/api.md and .codewiki/kb/system/roadmap.md describe wait entries, heartbeat/TTL, ready state, and artifact-first resume.
.codewiki/kb/system/graph.md requires pending/ready waiter and blocker visibility.

summary: Documented claim wait queue semantics for API, roadmap coordination, and graph observability; TASK-074 was refined to include the wait queue executable delta.
knowledge_changes: .codewiki/kb/system/api.md: claim API now documents wait entries, TTL/heartbeat, ready wake signals, and artifact-first resume.
.codewiki/kb/system/roadmap.md: roadmap/parallel coordination now defines wait entries as runtime leases that become ready when blocking write claims clear.
.codewiki/kb/system/graph.md: graph invariants now include pending/ready waiters and waiter blocker observability.
roadmap_changes: Refined TASK-074 with claim wait queue acceptance and verification because it overlaps the existing loop/session isolation and session-handoff coordination task.
